title: Cellulitis
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Cellulitis is a skin infection treated with antibiotics when a clinician determines they are needed.
Patients should monitor for worsening redness, pain, drainage, fever, or other signs of worsening infection.
Patients with more severe infection or immune system problems may need higher-acuity care.
